text.title: Android Keystore and Signing Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the Android Keystore and Signing methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale. Hard rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: One signing decision per artefact; never batch Play App Signing vs self-managed across multiple apps in one record.
rule.rationale: Batching turns a per-app risk decision into a generic policy; each app's blast radius differs.

rule.statement: Every input (keystore path, alias, vault path) is typed with source; free-form prose is rejected.
rule.rationale: Untyped inputs cause silent CI breakages when paths drift; typed inputs are validated at config-load.

rule.statement: Single named owner accountable for key rotation and incident response; 'platform team' is not a valid owner.
rule.rationale: Plural ownership = no ownership; rotation deadlines slip without a named human.

rule.statement: Signing config carries semver + last_reviewed; configs older than 180 days flagged on read.
rule.rationale: Stale signing configs survive past the context they were designed for; freshness gating catches this.

rule.statement: debug.keystore MUST be per-developer and never reused as upload key; CI MUST never see debug.keystore.
rule.rationale: Conflating debug and release keys leads to non-recoverable lockouts when the developer leaves; CI exposure leaks the key into logs.
